title: Crispy Portuguese Cookie Fritters
description: Tangy, peppery cookies battered and fried until golden, served hot with a squeeze of lemon for a quick, crispy snack.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, paprika, and pepper.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg and stir in the milk and melted butter.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined; the batter should be thick but pourable.
Heat the oil in a deep skillet or pot over medium-high (around 180°C/350°F) until hot; test with a small drop of batter—it should sizzle immediately.
Drop heaping teaspoons of batter into the oil, spacing them about 2 inches apart; do not overcrowd.
Fry for 2–3 minutes on each side until golden brown and crisp; use tongs to flip.
Remove fritters with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve hot, optionally dusted with a pinch of sea salt and accompanied by a wedge of lemon.
Repeat with remaining batter; keep finished fritters warm in a 90°C/200°F oven if desired.
